A Space of One's Own.name: 나만의 공간 - A Space of One's Own
A Space of One's Own.description:
Starting at 14th level, you learn to use the Broken World as a wicked gaol. You can cast the @Compendium[dnd5e.spells.pxpb2eOB6bv4phAf]{Banishment} spell once targeting a number of creatures up to your Charisma modifier (minimum of 크리처 하나), without expending a spell slot. A target banished this way is sent to your mastered space within the Broken World for the spell’s duration, regardless of its native plane of existence. The target is incapacitated and restrained, immune to all damage, and can breathe normally. At the end of the duration, you can choose to eject the target into the Broken World.

Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a long rest

Avatar of Just Sedition.name: Avatar 의 - Just Sedition
Avatar of Just Sedition.description:
At 20th level, you can call forth the song of insurrection against oppression. As an action, you gain the following benefits for 1 minute:

  • You can't be blinded, paralyzed, petrified, or stunned.
  • You gain a flying speed equal to your walking speed, and can hover.
  • Your voice booms up to three times as loud as normal.
  • You can cast the spell @Compendium[dnd5e.spells.T1vpZLam7LezjToj]{Divine Word} once per turn without expending a spell slot.
  • Each friendly creature that can see you can reroll one attack roll, saving throw, or ability check once during the duration.

Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

Bonus Proficiencies.name: 추가 숙련 - Bonus Proficiencies
Bonus Proficiencies.description: 3레벨에 전승 학파를 선택하면, 원하는 기술 3개에 숙련됩니다.

Branded Sigil.description:
At 9th level, you create a set of three iron brands that each burn a different two-inch demonic sigil into objects and creatures. You can have a number of sigils active at a time equal to your proficiency bonus.

You can use a bonus action to make a special melee attack against a creature or object. Make a Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) check against the target’s AC. On a hit, you deal 1d2 necrotic damage and burn a sigil into the target if it takes 피해. Choose one of the following effects:


@UUID[Compendium.beast-world.class-features.Item.PBjkHQD1DKkw2XCR]{Ggur (rot)}. When branded onto an object, this sigil slowly eats it away until it is destroyed. The object takes 2d6 necrotic damage when you brand the object and at the start of every subsequent round, as it decays into ash. If the object is Huge or larger, the sigil damages a 5-foot cube of material centered on itself. The sigil can be removed by using an action to deal 5 damage to the object, destroying the part of the object bearing the brand. Otherwise, the sigil lasts until the object is destroyed, or until the following dawn.

@UUID[Compendium.beast-world.class-features.Item.YVY1485bYaBddBGi]{Saa (whisper)}. After burning this mark into an object, you can use a bonus action to possess its physical form or return your consciousness to your own body. While possessing the object, you can see and hear as if you were in its space, and speak with a disembodied voice. While you are possessing the object, you are blinded and deafened in regard to your own surroundings. This sigil lasts until the following dawn.

@UUID[Compendium.beast-world.class-features.Item.fLDW3aWhUOWEZbvH]{Chot (curse)}. When a creature is burned by this brand, it takes extra damage from attacks equal to half your rogue level (rounded down) for the next minute. If a creature uses an action to cut the sigil off of the creature, it takes 5 slashing damage and the effect ends.

Carrion Master Spells.name: 송장술 달인 - Spells
Carrion Master Spells.description:
Starting at 3rd level, you learn an additional spell when you reach certain levels in this class, as shown in the Carrion Master Spells table. The spell counts as a ranger spell for you, but it doesn’t count against the number of ranger spells you know.

Ranger Level

Spells

3rd

@Compendium[dnd5e.spells.7e3QXF10hLNDEdr6]{False Life}

5th

@UUID[Compendium.beast-world.spells.Item.Fih6H2YzDrl9Xrsr]{Grim Vitality}

9th

@Compendium[dnd5e.spells.oyE5nVppa5mde5gT]{Animate Dead}

13th

@Compendium[dnd5e.spells.VtCXMdyM6mAdIJZb]{Death Ward}

17th

@Compendium[dnd5e.spells.mbFw57uyfLkyiw5k]{Seeming}

Channel Divinity: Compel Obeisance.name: 신성 변환: - Compel Obeisance
Channel Divinity: Compel Obeisance.description:
As an action, you present your holy symbol and invoke the eminence of your deity, using your Channel Divinity. A creature you can see within 60 feet of you must make a Charisma saving throw. A target automatically succeeds on this saving throw if it can’t be charmed. A target who creates or enforces laws in their community (such as a mayor, judge, or lord) has disadvantage on this saving throw.

내성 굴림에 실패하면, the creature falls prone, and cannot stand for 1 minute. At the start of each of your turns for the next minute, you can ask the target a question that it must answer truthfully and to the best of its ability. Each time the target takes damage, it can make another Charisma saving throw. 내성 굴림에 성공하면, or once the effect ends, the target becomes immune to this feature for the next 24 hours.

Demonstrative Destruction.description: Beginning at 6th level, you can reap violence that strikes an observer to their visceral depths. When you score a critical hit with a melee weapon attack, you can choose a different creature within 60 feet of you that can see you. That creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw with a DC equal to 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Constitution modifier or take psychic damage equal to half the damage dealt (rounded down).

Devil's Tongue.description: Starting at 9th level, you bear the countenance and beguiling nature of a devil, even when not wearing the Facade of the Fiend. You automatically pass saving throws to resist the @Compendium[dnd5e.spells.CylBa7jR8DSbo8Z3]{Zone of Truth} spell. Additionally, creatures have disadvantage on ability checks made to recognize lies you speak or write.

Evoke Eclipse (14th).description:
Starting at 14th level, you gain Aubade’s blood favor when you act fearlessly in combat. When you deal fire or cold damage to a hostile creature within 5 feet of you with a spell, you enter the state of Eclipse. Until the start of your next turn, you gain the following benefits:

  • Your movement speed is doubled.
  • You gain 10 temporary hit points.
  • You gain an additional action that can be used only to take the Attack (one weapon attack only), Dash, Disengage, Hide, or Use an Object action.
  • Your melee weapon attacks deal an extra 4 fire or cold damage (your choice). Targets take an extra 4 cold damage from your spells that deal fire damage, and vice versa.
  • You have advantage on your first melee weapon attack you make on your turn.

Extra Attack.name: 추가 공격 - Extra Attack
Extra Attack.description:
당신은 자기 턴에 공격 행동을 할 때마다 공격을 두 번씩 할 수 있습니다.

가할 수 있는 공격 횟수는 파이터 11레벨에 3번, 20레벨에 4번으로 늘어납니다.

Finisher: Shooting Star Press.name: Finisher: Shooting 별 - Press
Finisher: Shooting Star Press.description:
You jump into a backflip on your way into your dive, driving the force of your impact straight into an enemy. As an action you can spend 3 Heat to make a high jump, covering double the normal distance vertically and without spending movement. If you aren’t Tiny and fall at least 20 feet into a Small or larger creature’s space, it must make a Dexterity saving throw. 내성 굴림에 실패하면, you take no fall damage, and the target takes damage equal to the damage resulting from the fall + your fighter level + your Dexterity bonus. The target is also knocked prone unless it’s more than two size categories larger than you. 내성 굴림에 성공하면, you take half the damage resulting from the fall. Either way, you land in the nearest unoccupied space.

Foundry note

Fall damage is rolled separately

Gimmick: High-Flier.description:
The high-flier is focused on hurtling their body through the air in high-risk dives to destroy enemies and thrill everyone else. You gain a climbing speed equal to your walking speed. Additionally, when you jump from climbing a surface too steep to stand on, you can travel up to half your speed horizontally without spending movement if you fall at least 10 feet.

@UUID[Compendium.beast-world.class-features.Item.ic4jgEkfUS29T16h]{Spot: Splashing Attack}. You can use a bonus action to spread your body wide and dive into a creature. You make a high jump, covering double the normal distance vertically and without spending movement. If you aren’t Tiny and fall at least 20 feet into a Small or larger creature’s space, take damage from the fall as normal and make a melee weapon attack against the creature. On a hit, you deal an extra 2d6 damage and draw 2 heat. You then land in the nearest unoccupied space.

@UUID[Compendium.beast-world.class-features.Item.C1asD2DXZErD5T6l]{Finisher: Shooting Star Press}. You jump into a backflip on your way into your dive, driving the force of your impact straight into an enemy. As an action you can spend 3 Heat to make a high jump, covering double the normal distance vertically and without spending movement. If you aren’t Tiny and fall at least 20 feet into a Small or larger creature’s space, it must make a Dexterity saving throw. 내성 굴림에 실패하면, you take no fall damage, and the target takes damage equal to the damage resulting from the fall + your fighter level + your Dexterity bonus. The target is also knocked prone unless it’s more than two size categories larger than you. 내성 굴림에 성공하면, you take half the damage resulting from the fall. Either way, you land in the nearest unoccupied space.

Gimmick: Powerhouse.description:
The powerhouse is an unstoppable force that walks through battle as blows glance off of their mighty form. You have advantage on saving throws made to resist being moved or knocked prone. Additionally, when using the Second Wind feature, you can treat a d10 roll of 6 or lower as 7.

@UUID[Compendium.beast-world.class-features.Item.6YpRvP5MStsgS52g]{Spot: No Sell}. You invite your pitiful attackers to show you their wrath, and the energy of the fight itself demands they answer. As an action, you can goad foes into taking a “free shot.” Choose a number of creatures within 60 feet of you who can see or hear you, up to a number equal to your proficiency bonus. Each target must make a Wisdom saving throw. A target automatically passes this save if it can’t be charmed. 내성 굴림에 실패하면, a target must attempt to make at least one weapon attack against you during its next turn. If it can’t do so, it does nothing. Until the start of your next turn, you have resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage and the first time a creature makes a weapon attack against you, you draw 2 Heat.

@UUID[Compendium.beast-world.class-features.Item.MLeA7YajbzLFxmdd]{Finisher: Bulldozer. }You channel the awe of the crowd to trample all who stand in your way. At the start of your turn, you can spend 3 Heat. Until the end of your turn, you can move through a creature’s space once if it is no more than one size category larger than you, treating it as normal terrain. If you do, the creature must succeed on a Strength saving throw or take damage equal to your Fighter level.

Gimmick: Technician.description:
The technician is a master of grappling, maneuvering limbs to constrict enemies in new and creative ways. Once per round on your turn, when you hit a creature within your reach with a melee weapon attack, you can also attempt to grapple them. Additionally, you don’t need a free hand to attempt a grapple.

@UUID[Compendium.beast-world.class-features.Item.fQiFj0RZJTNb9s9U]{Pinning Setup}. While grappling a creature, as an action you can attempt to pin it into a more compromising position. 대상은 근력 내성 굴림을 해야 합니다. 내성 굴림에 실패하면, you draw 2 Heat. The target can’t cast spells with somatic components and has disadvantage on attack rolls until it escapes the grapple. Additionally, until the target escapes the grapple, you take only half the damage dealt to you, and the target takes the other half. You can only have 크리처 하나 pinned at a time.

@UUID[Compendium.beast-world.class-features.Item.dfAOYZJyIp1djS7d]{Finisher: Boot Squad}. While grappling a creature, you can use your action and spend 3 Heat to call allies for a coordinated beatdown. Each friendly creature within 30 feet of you can immediately use its reaction to move up to its speed, without provoking opportunity attacks. Then, you and each friendly creature within 5 feet of a creature you are grappling can make a weapon attack against the grappled creature. These attacks don’t cause you to draw Heat.

Grafted Sting.description:
Grafting a scorpion's tail to your companion grants it the following ability:

Sting (recharge 5-6). 근접 무기 공격: proficiency bonus + Strength modifier 명중, 사거리 5피트, 크리처 하나. Hit: 1d10 + the companion’s Strength modifier + your proficiency bonus piercing damage, and the target must make a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC, 실패 시 4d10 poison damage를 받고, 성공 시 그 절반의 피해만 받습니다.

Foundry Note:

Edit this feature's saving throw DC to match yours.

Grafted Web.description:
This graft requires the corpse of a giant spider. Your companion gains the following action:

Web (recharge 5—6). 원거리 무기 공격: proficiency bonus + Dexterity modifier 명중, 사거리 30/60피트, 크리처 하나. Hit: The target is restrained by webbing. As an action, the restrained target can make a Strength check against your spell save DC, bursting the webbing on a success. The webbing can also be attacked and destroyed (AC 10; hp 5; vulnerability to fire damage; immunity to bludgeoning, poison, and psychic damage).

Haunted Consort.description:
After joining at 3rd level, the College of Witches trains a bard to attract ghosts in the Netherworld with a performance. A witch can then consort with ghosts to exchange attention for power. As part of a long rest, your eyes become cloudy and gray as you look into the Netherworld and perform for a ghostly audience. Your GM determines how many ghosts are in your audience according to the Haunted Audience Table.

Haunted Audience

Location

Ghosts

Deserted. Rarely sees willful creatures.

3

Quiet. Life is uncomplicated.

4

Populous. Thousands gather and live here.

5

Haunted. Sudden, strong emotions or persistent repetition are common.

6

Woefully haunted. There are horror stories about this place.

7

Roll a d6 for each ghost in your audience, based on your location. The results determine each ghost's capabilties according to the Ghost Essence Table.

At the end of a long rest, you can also make a DC 15 [[/r 1d20 + @skills.prf.mod]]{Charisma (Performance)} check, attracting one additional ghost of an essence you choose on a success. Starting at 13th level, success attracts two ghosts with an essence you choose.

Ghost Essence

d6

Essence

1-2

@UUID[Compendium.beast-world.class-features.Item.XllKicoIKfYHBmak]{The Steward}

3-4

@UUID[Compendium.beast-world.class-features.Item.zD9rURrWONMvCRqS]{The Scoundrel}

5-6

@UUID[Compendium.beast-world.class-features.Item.GyOz41dQXG0MM0cm]{The Scholar}

Choose a number of ghosts equal to your proficiency bonus from your audience. Until the start of your next long rest, each haunts a friendly creature you choose within 60 feet of you (multiple ghosts can haunt 크리처 하나).

You can use a bonus action to spend Bardic Inspiration to manifest a ghost haunting a creature you can see within 60 feet of you for 1 minute. Each ghost confers abilities while manifested, dependent on its Essence. At the end of the manifestation, a ghost haunting a creature other than you has fulfilled its end of the compact and disappears back into the Netherworld. A ghost haunting you remains, and can be manifested again.

Foundry Note

To haunt one of your friends, add the relevant essence to their inventory. Equip it when manifested to bestow its benefits.

Heat.description:
Heat is the primal force of combat, its raw momentum and thrill. You draw (gain) Heat with dramatic fighting, and spend it to perform feats of theatrical prowess. Starting when you take this archetype at 3rd level, when you roll initiative at the start of combat, you have 0 Heat. You can never have more than 3 Heat (any excess is lost).

You immediately draw 1 Heat if:

• A creature scores a critical hit against you.

• You are reduced to 5 or fewer hit points. This increases to 10 or fewer at 6th level, 15 or fewer at 11th level, and 20 or fewer at 16th level.

• A creature lifts you at least 10 feet in the air.

• You hit with an attack roll made with disadvantage.

• You escape from being swallowed by a creature.

• You shove or throw a creature into a fall of at least 10 feet.

• A friendly creature hits a creature you are grappling with an attack.

• You hit with an improvised weapon attack. You can only draw Heat from an improvised weapon that deals the same type of damage once until you finish a long rest.

Appeal: Once per round, if you haven’t drawn Heat from another source, you can replace an attack with a dramatic show of confidence, called an Appeal. Make a DC 15 Charisma (Performance) check. On a success, you draw 1 Heat.

Saving Throws. Some of your abilities require your target to make a saving throw to resist their effects. The saving throw DC for your spots is 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength or Dexterity modifier (your choice).

Foundry Note:

Use this feature to track all the various uses of Heat for the fighter.

Inverted Pilgrimage.description:
Starting at 1st level, while holding a nonmagical object bearing your likeness (such as a figurine or drawing), you can use your action to shift yourself to the Broken World. Your likeness stays behind in your space.

The dangerous landscape of the Broken World doesn’t harm you (for more information about its different environments, see The Broken World on page 182). Time flows normally, the temperature is comfortable, and the air is breathable. However, you aren’t exempt from dangers other than the atmosphere that exist in the Broken World.

While in the Broken World, you can use your action to transport yourself to the unoccupied space closest to the likeness you left behind. If your likeness is destroyed, you are stranded until you find a way home.

While you are not in the Broken World, any nonmagical objects from other worlds that you leave there will slowly decay. A non-native object takes 1 necrotic damage every hour while left behind in the Broken World.

As part of a long rest spent in the Broken World, you can walk the landscape and gather material components for all the spells you know, which are “gifts” left by your patron. These components are suitable nonliving facsimiles of the usual sort, such as flowers made of wire and broken glass.

You gain a +1 bonus to spell attack rolls and to the saving throw DCs of your warlock spells cast using components from the Broken World. They can’t substitute spell components with a listed gold piece value and disintegrate 24 hours after leaving the Broken World.

Warlocks looking to avoid their patron’s feedback should think before traveling to the Broken World. While a warlock is in their domain, the Ghost God can speak directly to them. However, one must balance time spent away. After 7 days without entering the Broken World, you are wracked with nightmares and headaches. Every day at dawn thereafter, you take psychic damage equal to 1d6 + your warlock level. This damage cannot be prevented by any means.

Where Do I End Up?

A Ghost God warlock’s point of arrival is always the same: usually a vast, empty landscape with nothing more than scattered ruins in the distance. It may be a tract in the Bleeding Fields, an island in the Glass Sea, or simply a flat, unnamed expanse of black marble under a white sky.

Kidney Punch.description: When you choose this tradition at 3rd level, you learn to shock an enemy’s system with ki, leaving them gasping for breath. When you hit another creature with an unarmed strike, you can spend 1 ki point to attempt a Kidney Punch. 대상은 건강 내성 굴림을 해야 합니다. 내성 굴림에 실패하면, the target suffers one level of exhaustion.

Lucid Daydream.name: Lucid 백일몽
Lucid Daydream.description:
At 10th level, you can enter a deep dream of your surroundings, affecting the real world while asleep. You can use your bonus action to fall unconscious. While unconscious in this way, you can’t move, take actions, and automatically fail Strength and Dexterity saving throws. However, the following exceptions apply:

  • Your dream keeps you aware of your surroundings, and you can maintain concentration on ongoing spells.
  • You can speak.
  • You have blindsight out to 60 feet.

Additionally, while in this dreaming state you can take actions, bonus actions, and reactions to cast spells, ignoring somatic components and material components without an indicated cost. Transmutation spells you cast don’t require concentration while you are asleep, and can be dismissed at any time.

Lucid Daydream lasts a number of minutes equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum one). You can awaken early as a bonus action. Any ongoing transmutation spells you cast while asleep end when you awaken. After using this feature, you can’t do so again until you finish a long rest, unless you expend a spell slot of 5th level or higher to enter the dream state again.

Metamagic: Penetrating Spell.name: Metamagic: Penetrating 주문
Metamagic: Penetrating Spell.description:
You see weakness where others see immunity. You gain the Penetrating Spell Metamagic option:

When you cast a spell that deals damage, you can spend 2 sorcery points to treat targeted creatures’ immunity to a damage type as resistance, and ignore resistance for the purposes of determining the spell’s 피해.

Patronage of Space.name: Patronage 의 - Space
Patronage of Space.description:
At 10th level, with regular travel to the Broken World, you’ve gradually mastered its unpredictable physics. You gain total control over a 120-foot cube of space in the Broken World. Objects within this space no longer take 피해.

When you access your space with your Inverse Pilgrimage feature, you can leave behind a free-standing circular portal 5 to 10 feet wide along with your likeness. Creatures and objects can move through the portal as long as you keep it open. It remains open for 10 minutes, or until you close it as an action within 5 feet of it on either side.

With 8 hours of work within your space, you can change the following properties:

  • Boundaries. Your space can have physical continuity with the larger Broken World or it can be a discrete pocket with inky black walls.
  • Non-living, nonmagical matter. Objects created in your space decay into ash moments after being removed from the Broken World. Your space cannot sustain life—any seeds sown in your realm are doomed.
  • Atmosphere. Temperature, wind, light, and gravity are under your control. You can even designate specific creatures who are able to breathe in your otherwise airless space.

Mastery of a physical space has a second benefit.
Also starting at 10th level, you are a patron. You can create your own pact, even as you continue to serve the Ghost God. With an 8-hour ritual in your space (you determine the specifics, it’s your ritual), you form a pact with your chosen vassal. This creature must enter your service willingly. When the ritual finishes, your servant becomes a 1st-level Ghost God Warlock, except they answer to you and their Inverse Pilgrimage leads them to your mastered space. When you gain a level, your warlock’s maximum level increases by one (your warlock can reach 2nd level when you reach 11th level, and so on).

The GM portrays the NPC, unless they wish to give you control over them. Most warlocks will agree to spend at most a few days a month taking direct orders from you. They won’t place themselves or their loved ones in obvious mortal danger for you. Remember that they are their own person. Most warlocks are in the profession for themselves, not you. The Ghost God is flexible about how you behave as a patron. However, use caution when commanding your warlock to betray their wishes. Your underling’s behavior is your responsibility in the eyes of the Ghost God.

If the warlock displeases you, you can perform an 8-hour ritual to decrease their warlock level by one or more, which can be done during a long rest. They cannot regain the levels or gain new ones until you release the limitation as an action. You can continue to decrease their warlock level with subsequent rituals—a warlock with 0 levels has their pact severed, and no longer serves you. If you perform the pact ritual with a different creature, your previous pact is severed.

Profane Graft.description:
Starting at 11th level, as part of a short or long rest, you can graft parts from the preserved corpse of a Beast onto your companion if the corpse has been dead for less than one year. Add one of the following traits to your companion or replace an existing one. The corpse used to harvest the parts needed for the graft is destroyed in the process. At 15th level, your companion can have two of these traits at once.

Scorpion Tail. This graft requires the corpse of a giant scorpion. Your companion gains the following action:

  • @UUID[Compendium.beast-world.class-features.Item.UcTKHAZnMfQhI70f]{Sting} (recharge 5-6). 근접 무기 공격: proficiency bonus + Strength modifier 명중, 사거리 5피트, 크리처 하나. Hit: 1d10 + the companion’s Strength modifier + your proficiency bonus piercing damage, and the target must make a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC, 실패 시 4d10 poison damage를 받고, 성공 시 그 절반의 피해만 받습니다.

@UUID[Compendium.beast-world.class-features.Item.Jc52GWg01x8iR1yJ]{Spider Legs}. This graft requires the corpse of a giant spider. Your companion gains a climb speed of 30 feet, if it doesn’t already have one.

Web Sac. This graft requires the corpse of a giant spider. Your companion gains the following action:

  • @UUID[Compendium.beast-world.class-features.Item.EDDnqEPd5P3UohQN]{Web} (recharge 5—6). 원거리 무기 공격: proficiency bonus + Dexterity modifier 명중, 사거리 30/60피트, 크리처 하나. Hit: The target is restrained by webbing. As an action, the restrained target can make a Strength check against your spell save DC, bursting the webbing on a success. The webbing can also be attacked and destroyed (AC 10; hp 5; vulnerability to fire damage; immunity to bludgeoning, poison, and psychic damage).

@UUID[Compendium.beast-world.class-features.Item.kotbgaYMjRAN8zYp]{Webbed Feet}. This graft requires the corpse of a giant toad. Your companion gains a swimming speed of 30 feet.

@UUID[Compendium.beast-world.class-features.Item.EY7FZhbrlp876du4]{Wings}. This graft requires the corpse of a Medium or larger flying beast. Your companion gains a flying speed of 40 feet, if it doesn’t already have one.

Shared Precision.description:
Starting at 14th level, your mastery of emotions is
complete, and your allies can join in the reverberations of your anger. As part of the bonus action you take to enter your rage, you can cast the @Compendium[dnd5e.spells.uAwtVZkiSTyP6ORB]{Telepathic Bond} spell, requiring no components. Until the end of your rage, other creatures bonded to you by the spell can add your Constitution modifier to the first attack roll they make in a round. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for this spell, and it lasts its full duration.

Sigil of Death's Defiance.name: Sigil 의 죽음's - Defiance
Sigil of Death's Defiance.description:
Inevitably, a master Fell Infiltrator undertakes a mission that no creature could hope to return from. But their work continues nonetheless. At 17th level, you gain access to a fourth branded sigil:

Brust (defiance). After burning this mark on an object, one week after you die for any reason except old age, your body and any equipment still on your person teleport to the nearest unoccupied space from the sigil, unharmed.

You return to life at full hit points. Every time you resurrect this way, your Hit Dice are reduced by 1. Only the direct intervention of a deity can restore these lost Hit Dice. If you have 0 Hit Dice, you die permanently.

This sigil can only exist in one place. If you brand it on another object, the previous sigil is erased. It can be removed by using an action to deal 5 damage to the object, destroying the part of the object bearing the brand.

Spot: Clothesline.description: As a reaction to a creature entering your reach, you can spend 1 Heat to make a melee weapon attack against that creature. On a hit, the target must make a Strength saving throw. 내성 굴림에 실패하면, the target is knocked prone and its speed is reduced to 0 until the start of your next turn.

Spot: No Sell.description: You invite your pitiful attackers to show you their wrath, and the energy of the fight itself demands they answer. As an action, you can goad foes into taking a “free shot.” Choose a number of creatures within 60 feet of you who can see or hear you, up to a number equal to your proficiency bonus. Each target must make a Wisdom saving throw. A target automatically passes this save if it can’t be charmed. 내성 굴림에 실패하면, a target must attempt to make at least one weapon attack against you during its next turn. If it can’t do so, it does nothing. Until the start of your next turn, you have resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage and the first time a creature makes a weapon attack against you, you draw 2 Heat.

Spot: Perfect Reversal.description: As a reaction to a creature missing you with a weapon attack, you can spend 2 Heat to make a weapon attack against that creature. This attack scores a critical hit on a roll of 17-20.

Spot: Pinning Setup.description: While grappling a creature, as an action you can attempt to pin it into a more compromising position. 대상은 근력 내성 굴림을 해야 합니다. 내성 굴림에 실패하면, you draw 2 Heat. The target can’t cast spells with somatic components and has disadvantage on attack rolls until it escapes the grapple. Additionally, until the target escapes the grapple, you take only half the damage dealt to you, and the target takes the other half. You can only have 크리처 하나 pinned at a time.

Spots.description:
Also at 3rd level, you gain the ability to release the tension of drawn Heat with spectacular maneuvers. Choose 3 Spots. You can choose an additional Spot at 7th, 10th, and 15th level.

@UUID[Compendium.beast-world.class-features.Item.t9ISlEtfn8OGye3Y]{Spot: Kip Up} As a reaction to being knocked prone, you can spend 1 Heat to stand up and make a melee weapon attack against the creature that knocked you prone.

@UUID[Compendium.beast-world.class-features.Item.pdbd7SOi8btfL8WV]. As a reaction to a creature entering your reach, you can spend 1 Heat to make a melee weapon attack against that creature. On a hit, the target must make a Strength saving throw. 내성 굴림에 실패하면, the target is knocked prone and its speed is reduced to 0 until the start of your next turn.

@UUID[Compendium.beast-world.class-features.Item.UUlfcnq1SnFTfvf8]{Spot: Hot Tag}. As an action, you can spend 2 Heat to touch a creature other than yourself. The creature can immediately use its reaction to move up to its speed, without provoking opportunity attacks. As it moves, it can make one melee weapon attack against each creature within reach, dealing extra damage equal to half your fighter level (rounded down) on a hit.

@UUID[Compendium.beast-world.class-features.Item.ZueQZmOR003EDaJw]{Spot: Perfect Reversal} As a reaction to a creature missing you with a weapon attack, you can spend 2 Heat to make a weapon attack against that creature. This attack scores a critical hit on a roll of 17-20.

@UUID[Compendium.beast-world.class-features.Item.ehSskeyybQZIcOhl]{Spot: Vital Throw}. You can use a bonus action and spend 2 Heat to throw a grappled creature up to 15 feet horizontally. The target and creatures in the space it lands in must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take bludgeoning damage equal to your fighter level + your Strength modifier and fall prone. If the space is occupied, the thrown creature lands in the nearest unoccupied space you choose.

@UUID[Compendium.beast-world.class-features.Item.kduRfwuvC2xwFThj]{Spot: Wrenching Lock}. When you make a melee weapon attack against a creature you are grappling, you can spend 2 Heat. On a hit, you add damage equal to your fighter level + your Strength modifier, and the target has disadvantage on ability checks to escape the grapple until the end of their next turn.

Sun Bull's Tenacity.name: 태양 - Bull's Tenacity
Sun Bull's Tenacity.description:
Starting at 1st level, the rush of Sunblood slows the world around you and makes a grievous wound an afterthought. Being within 5 feet of a hostile creature doesn’t impose disadvantage on your ranged spell attack rolls.

Additionally, you can shrug off a blow that would leave less stubborn creatures unconscious. As a reaction to being reduced to 0 hit points, you can stay conscious until the end of your next turn (you still roll death saving throws as normal). Once you use this ability, you can’t use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

The Dread Compromise Spreads.name: 두려운 타협의 확산 - The Dread Compromise Spreads
The Dread Compromise Spreads.description:
As you grow in power, the dark whispers that command your companion also affect other undead. Starting at 7th level, you can use your action to raise the corpse of a Medium or Small Humanoid or Beast you touch as a zombie under your control, or bring a zombie within 30 feet under your control (your GM has the creature’s game statistics). You can only control one zombie at a time with this feature.

Additionally, your companion’s attacks now count as magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and 피해.

The Scholar.name: 학자 - The Scholar
The Scholar.description:
When The Steward manifests, the haunted creature gains a +1 bonus to saving throws for the duration. This bonus increases to +2 at 9th level, and +3 at 15th level.

As a reaction to The Scholar manifesting, the haunted creature can activate a held magic item if it can normally activate the item with an action or bonus action. If the magic item regains charges at dawn, this activation costs one fewer charge. While The Scholar is manifested, the haunted creature can cast the @UUID[Compendium.dnd5e.spells.Item.ctW81uiX56xZR2c5]{ray of frost} cantrip. Charisma is its spellcasting ability for the spell. Additionally, cantrips cast by the haunted creature that deal damage deal an extra [[/r 1d6]] force 피해.

The Scholar can translate magic secrets. If the haunted creature holds a magic scroll for the manifestation’s full duration, black flames immolate the scroll, destroying it and causing the haunted creature 1d3 fire 피해. The haunted creature is left holding a scroll of the same spell. The haunted creature (and only the haunted creature) can cast the spell on this scroll, ignoring all class, race, and level requirements.

Foundry Note:

A haunted creature can equip this item when the ghost inside is manifested to activate its bonuses to saving throws.

Adjust the values in the effect as you level.

The Scoundrel.name: 악한 - The Scoundrel
The Scoundrel.description:
This ghost possesses an item the haunted creature chooses on its person. The haunted creature can use an action to order The Scoundrel to possess a different item. Items remain possessed at any distance, until a new one is chosen or the haunting ends.

When The Scoundrel manifests, the haunted creature gains a +1 bonus to ability checks for the duration. This bonus increases to +2 at 9th level, and +3 at 15th level.

While manifested, the haunted creature senses the direction and distance of the possessed item. The item is visible to the haunted creature through solid objects, appearing as a faint gray outline. Weapon attacks made by the haunted creature using the possessed item deal an extra [[/r 1d6]] force damage on a hit.

While holding the possessed item, the haunted creature can use a bonus action to order The Scoundrel to hide it. The item disappears, following the haunted creature from the Netherworld. The haunted creature can use a bonus action to make it reappear in its hand.

Foundry Note:

A haunted creature can equip this item when the ghost inside is manifested to activate its bonuses to ability checks.

Adjust the values in the effect as you level.

The Steward.name: 청지기 - The Steward
The Steward.description:
When The Steward manifests, the haunted creature gains a +1 bonus to attack rolls for the duration. This bonus increases to +2 at 9th level, and +3 at 15th level. Additionally, The Steward materializes in front of the haunted creature as a floating shield of black stone. The creature is treated as if wielding an animated shield it is proficient with.

As a bonus action, the haunted creature can command The Steward to transform into a wall of stone up to 10 feet on a side and 6 inches thick, at a point within 60 feet the haunted creature can see. This wall has AC 15 and 30 hit points. The haunted creature can use a bonus action within 15 feet of it to transform it back into an animated shield and wield it.

Foundry Note:

A haunted creature can equip this item when the ghost inside is manifested to activate its bonuses to attacks and AC.

Adjust the values in the effect as you level.

Wild Card.name: Wild 카드
Wild Card.description:
After joining this circle at 2nd level, you craft the Wild Cards, a deck of 24 cards that channel the blessings of the god nature and chaos. If the deck is lost, you can craft another with a ritual that takes 8 hours to perform.

As an action, you can draw a card from the deck by rolling a d2, a d3, and a d4 and consulting the @UUID[Compendium.beast-world.rules.JournalEntry.0Le30yi2jQD5P5px]{Wild Card Tables} . If you draw a second card before using the first, the unused card disappears back into the deck. The deck has only one of each card.

Once before you finish a long rest, you can use the drawn card. The required action and its effects are detailed in the “Wild Cards” at the end of the subclass description. Unless the card’s description says otherwise, using the card takes an action. You must be holding a card to use it, and you can’t use one while in beast form from the Wild Shape feature. The card disappears back into the deck after use.

You can draw a card from the deck three times, and you regain all expended draws when you finish a long rest. You can draw 4 times at 6th level, and 5 times at 14th level.

Saving Throws. Some of your cards require your target(s) to make a saving throw to resist the effect of the card. The saving throw DC is equal to your druid spell save DC.

Wild Card Tables.name: 와일드 카드 표 - Wild Card Tables
